source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/01_HAD1717MC(MG)/▶ CE MDD/인허가 준비 자료(초기)/5. Software validation/A-TCF-5-010_Software Validation_20190315.docx

document.title: Software Validation For X-ray Detector HAD1717MC, HAD1717MG
standards: FDA Quality System Regulation 21 CFR, Part 820
FDA Quality System Regulation 21 CFR, Part 803
CDRH Guidance: General Principles of Software Validation
CDRH Guidance: Off-The-Shelf Software Use in Medical Devices
CDRH Guidance: Guidance for the Submission of Premarket Notifications for Medical Image Management Devices
IEEE 1012-1986, Standard for Software Verification and Validation Plans
IEEE 829-1983, Standard for Software Test Documentation

sourceSummary.oneLine: Software validation report for the HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG X-ray detector acquisition library.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports software validation documentation, including level of concern, SRS, SDS, hazard analysis, test plan, traceability, and V&V results.
keyPoints: The software level of concern is defined as moderate.
The software is described as a middle-ware utility library between the still image detector and the End User Application.
Static and dynamic test results are recorded as approved or passed.
The software revision history lists version 1.0.0 dated March/15/2019 as the initial release.

entities.name: FDA
entities.evidence: FDA Quality System Regulation 21 CFR, Part 820

entities.name: CDRH
entities.evidence: CDRH Guidance: General Principles of Software Validation

entities.name: End-User Application (EUA)
entities.evidence: End-User Application (EUA) – upper level software that acquires images from detector using this library and then presents (displays) images to end-user.

concepts.name: Software Level of Concern
concepts.evidence: TFT-detectors use low X-ray irradiation that could not harm patient seriously in any way. Because of that the level of concern is defined as “moderate”.

concepts.name: Failure Mode and Effects Analysis
concepts.evidence: The model used for the Hazard Evaluation was Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA).

concepts.name: Verification and Validation Testing
concepts.evidence: Description of V&V activities at the unit, integration, and system level. System level test protocol, including pass/fail criteria, and tests results.

concepts.name: Waterfall Life Cycle Model
concepts.evidence: Software development process follows classic methodology of Waterfall Life Cycle Model

concepts.name: Bad Pixels Correction
concepts.evidence: Bad Pixels Correction This operation corrects scattered bad (dead) pixels by interpolation with good neighbors.

evidence.claim: The document is a software validation report for X-ray detector models HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG.
evidence.evidenceText: Software Validation For X-ray Detector HAD1717MC, HAD1717MG

evidence.claim: The report number and revision are A-TCF-5-010 Rev.0.
The document was initially issued on March/15/2019.
evidence.evidenceText: 0 March/15/2019 Initially issued to Software validation.

evidence.claim: The software level of concern is moderate.
evidence.evidenceText: Because of that the level of concern is defined as “moderate”.

evidence.claim: The software is a middle-ware utility library between the still image detector and the End User Application.
evidence.evidenceText: This is “middle-ware” utility library between still image detector (and its low-level driver) from one side and EUA from the other.

evidence.claim: The software includes SetUp functions for configuration, maintenance, Gain/Offset-Calibration, and Bad Pixels’ Correction.
evidence.evidenceText: It also contains SU to modify software and hardware configuration parameters and preparation of Gain/Offset-Calibration and Bad Pixels’ Correction.

evidence.claim: The development environment is Windows 10 Pro 64-bit with MS Visual C++ 2019 / MS Visual Studio Professional 2019.
evidence.evidenceText: Development Environment (SRS-C-DevE): Windows 10 pro 64bit. MS Visual C++ 2019 (MS Visual Studio Professional 2019).

evidence.claim: The deployment operating system requirement is at least Windows 7.
The hazard evaluation model is FMEA.
evidence.evidenceText: The model used for the Hazard Evaluation was Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA).

evidence.claim: Static verification was certified as properly accomplished.
evidence.evidenceText: As a result of the code review, we hereby certify that the static verification of Software is properly accomplished

evidence.claim: Dynamic tests found no unsolved bugs subject to safety and effectiveness.
evidence.evidenceText: There were no found unsolved bugs that are subjected to safety and effectiveness in this software.

evidence.claim: Software revision 1.0.0 was released on March/15/2019 as the initial release.
evidence.evidenceText: 1.0.0 March/15/2019 Initial release, Dual polynomial fitting calibration Only for proprietary applications.

reviewFlags.reason: Authority context is ambiguous: sourcePath indicates CE MDD, authoritySignal indicates FDA, and the document references FDA/CDRH regulations and guidances.
Approval and review signature fields are present as headings but the source text does not show completed review or approval names, dates, or signatures.
OCR/text quality issues are present, including misspellings such as Aril, Dnamic, Traceabilty, Sevice, and fragmented diagram text.
